question.label: The real version
question.name: Tell me about your week — the real version.
question.description: Kim Scott's 'care personally' made concrete. Signals that a performed answer won't cut it.

answer: yeah its okay, the recent team building was fun, but now with the New oe's it will be stressful
note: She reports a basically okay week with a fun team-building note, but anticipates stress from the new OEs — mild wellbeing drag with some positive engagement signal.

question.label: New OEs stress
question.name: With the new OEs, what looks stressful from your side?
question.description: Drills into the specific upcoming stressor she named so the manager can understand the real source of strain.

answer: the team building was great, but still feel i have issues with the BA team.
note: She names ongoing friction with the BA team as the stress source, which is a mild negative wellbeing signal; also reveals a concrete cross-team issue worth probing.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: team building great, — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: getting the BA team to understand that we need to be there with the PO's and then working with them.
note: She pivoted from the wellbeing probe to a concrete BA-team coordination issue with POs; that is off-signature work friction, especially clarity, worth picking up next.
